stories.title: Karlyn Pickens Leads Tennessee Into Super Regional Tonight (Tier 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tennessee is hosting a Super Regional for the FOURTH consecutive year.

No program in NCAA softball history has ever done that.

Karlyn Pickens takes the circle tonight vs. Georgia. 7 PM ET, ESPN2.

Knoxville is READY. 🥎 #LadyVols

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Tennessee vs. Georgia — tonight in Knoxville.

🏔️ Tennessee (45-10): 4th straight SR host, Pickens on the mound
🐾 Georgia (40-18): Fisher CG shutout in the Regional Final

Both pitchers are dealing. One team goes home this weekend. 7 PM ET, ESPN2. #LadyVols #GoDawgs

facebook.long_form:
🥎 TENNESSEE MAKES HISTORY TONIGHT 🥎

No program in NCAA softball history has hosted four consecutive Super Regionals.

Tonight, Tennessee does it for the first time.

Sherri Parker Lee Stadium in Knoxville is sold out. Karlyn Pickens — Tennessee's ace and one of the most dominant pitchers in the country this season — takes the circle at 7 PM ET on ESPN2. The Vols come in at 45-10 and have turned their home field into one of the most intimidating venues in college softball.

Standing in their way? Georgia. The Bulldogs are 40-18 and they bring Addisen Fisher — a pitcher who just threw a complete-game shutout against Clemson in the Regional Final. Fisher is not here to be an easy out.

But this is Tennessee's night. The crowd, the history, the moment — it all arrives together at 7 PM ET on ESPN2.

Four consecutive Super Regionals. First time it's ever been done.

🔷 7 PM ET | ESPN2 | Knoxville, TN

[Who do you think comes out of Knoxville? Tennessee or Georgia? Tell us below!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Tennessee makes NCAA softball history tonight — no program has ever hosted four consecutive Super Regionals. Karlyn Pickens takes the mound at Sherri Parker Lee Stadium as the Vols (45-10) host Georgia (40-18) in Game 1 at 7 PM ET on ESPN2. Knoxville is sold out. 🥎 #LadyVols

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Nebraska softball's first-ever Super Regional is TONIGHT.

49-6. 13 years since the WCWS. Sold-out crowd in Lincoln.

Jordy Frahm gets the ball. Oklahoma State stands in the way.

9 PM ET, ESPN2. 🌽 #Huskers

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Tonight in Lincoln:

Jordy Frahm (Nebraska, Lincoln native) vs. Ruby Neylan (OkState, Omaha native).

Former travel ball teammates. Now on opposite sides of the biggest night in Nebraska softball history.

9 PM ET, ESPN2. #Huskers #okstate

facebook.long_form:
🌽 THIRTEEN YEARS. TONIGHT IT ENDS. 🌽

Nebraska softball hasn't been to the Women's College World Series since 2013.

That drought ends this season — or it doesn't. And the answer starts tonight.

The Cornhuskers (49-6) host their first-ever Super Regional at Bob Devaney Sports Center in Lincoln. The building is sold out. The city has waited over a decade for this. Nebraska allowed just one run in the entire regional — that's dominant, methodical, program-defining softball.

And then there's the pitching matchup.

Jordy Frahm, a Lincoln native who grew up dreaming of this, is on the mound for Nebraska. She faces Ruby Neylan of Oklahoma State. Neylan is from Omaha. They were travel ball teammates once — two Nebraska kids who went separate directions and now find themselves on opposite sides of the most significant night in Cornhusker softball history.

Oklahoma State scored in double digits in all three of their regional games. They are not a pushover. But this Nebraska team hasn't lost more than six games all season, and tonight they play in front of a crowd that has poured its entire heart into this program.

13 years. One night. 9 PM ET on ESPN2.

[Are you watching tonight? Drop a 🌽 if you're rooting for Nebraska!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Nebraska opens its first-ever Super Regional TONIGHT — 13 years after their last WCWS trip. Jordy Frahm (Lincoln native) faces Ruby Neylan (Omaha native), her former travel ball teammate, now wearing Oklahoma State. 9 PM ET, ESPN2. 🌽 #Huskers

stories.title: Wells Enters Super Regional Stage — HR No. 38 Could Come Friday (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells needs 1 home run to own the NCAA single-season record outright.

She opens Super Regional play Friday vs. Mississippi State. 1 PM ET, ESPN2.

37 HRs. Tied with Espinoza (Arizona, 1995). One swing from history. 🥎 #Sooners

facebook.long_form:
🥎 ONE SWING FROM FOREVER — ROUND TWO 🥎

The record has been tied for a week. Oklahoma's Kendall Wells is sitting at 37 home runs — tied with Laura Espinoza of Arizona, who set the all-time NCAA single-season record in 1995.

Thirty-one years. That mark has stood untouched.

One home run separates Wells from owning it outright.

And now the stage just got bigger.

Oklahoma hosts Mississippi State in the Super Regional starting Friday, May 22 at 1 PM ET on ESPN2. Every at-bat in Norman this weekend carries history. Every pitch that Wells sees, the Mississippi State pitching staff knows exactly what's on the line — for Wells, for the record, and for themselves if they're the ones who served up No. 38.

How do you pitch to someone like that? You can try to walk her, but Oklahoma's offense is too dangerous to let that go on for a series. You can attack the zone and hope for the best. There's no great answer.

Wells isn't chasing the record. She's chasing the WCWS. The record is what happens when you're the most dangerous hitter in college softball playing at the highest level of the sport.

No. 38 could come Friday. Or Saturday. Or maybe not this weekend at all.

Every at-bat is must-watch. That's just Kendall Wells.

[Are you planning to tune in Friday? Let us know!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Kendall Wells is one home run from owning the all-time NCAA single-season HR record outright. She opens Super Regional play Friday vs. Mississippi State — 1 PM ET, ESPN2 in Norman. The stage is set. 🥎 #Sooners

stories.title: Georgia's Addisen Fisher Has Different Plans for Knoxville (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Georgia's Addisen Fisher threw a complete-game shutout in the Regional Final.

Tonight she goes to Knoxville — sold-out crowd, No. 7 Tennessee, and Karlyn Pickens on the other side.

If Georgia pulls the upset, it runs through Fisher. 7 PM ET, ESPN2. #GoDawgs

facebook.long_form:
🐾 GEORGIA IS NOT JUST HAPPY TO BE HERE 🐾

It would be easy to look at this series and assume Tennessee is going to win. They're 45-10, they're at home, they've hosted the Super Regional four years in a row, and the crowd at Sherri Parker Lee Stadium is going to be among the loudest in the country tonight.

But Georgia has Addisen Fisher.

Fisher walked into the Regional Final against Clemson and threw a complete-game shutout — 5-0 — on the biggest stage she'd been on all season. She was calm, commanding, and dominant when the Bulldogs needed it most.

That's not the profile of a team or a pitcher that's going to roll over in Knoxville. Fisher goes into hostile environments and shuts teams down. That's what she does.

Georgia is 40-18. They earned this Super Regional. And when Fisher is on, she has the ability to make any stadium go quiet.

Tennessee is the favorite. That's real. But tonight is a baseball game — played by human beings, on one specific night, with momentum and preparation and matchup factors that don't show up in a season record.

Georgia believes they can win. Addisen Fisher gives them a real reason to.

7 PM ET, ESPN2 — and it might be closer than anyone expects.

[Will Georgia pull the upset? Tell us your pick!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Georgia's Addisen Fisher threw a complete-game shutout in the Regional Final — and tonight she takes that form into sold-out Knoxville against No. 7 Tennessee. Don't count the Bulldogs out. 7 PM ET, ESPN2. 🐾 #GoDawgs

stories.title: Friday's Six Super Regional Openers — The Biggest Day of the NCAA Tournament (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Friday is the biggest day in college softball.

Super Regional openers:
11 AM: Florida-TTech (ESPN2), Arkansas-Duke (ESPNU)
1 PM: Oklahoma-Miss State (ESPN2)
7 PM: Alabama-LSU (ESPN2)
9 PM: UCF-UCLA (ESPNU), Texas-ASU (ESPN2)

All times ET. Devon Park OKC May 28. 🥎

facebook.long_form:
🥎 CLEAR YOUR FRIDAY SCHEDULE 🥎

You think Thursday is big? Thursday has two Super Regional games.

Friday has SIX.

It is the single largest day of the NCAA softball tournament — more simultaneous stakes, more storylines, more history-in-progress than any other day of the year. And every single one of these games matters.

Here's what's coming:

🕙 11 AM ET — Florida (51-10) vs. Texas Tech (55-6) | ESPN2
Florida hasn't lost a home NCAA game in 22 tries. Texas Tech won 55 games this season. Something has to give.

🕙 11 AM ET — Arkansas vs. Duke | ESPNU
Arkansas has been to 5 Super Regionals and never reached the WCWS. Duke is a Cinderella with nothing to lose.

🕐 1 PM ET — Oklahoma vs. Mississippi State | ESPN2
Kendall Wells needs one home run to break the all-time NCAA record. The world will be watching.

🕖 7 PM ET — Alabama (52-7) vs. LSU (40-17) | ESPN2
No. 1 meets No. 16. Alabama's pitching (Moten + Briski) against an LSU squad that outscored regional opponents 23-2.

🕙 9 PM ET — UCF vs. UCLA | ESPNU
UCF — who upset No. 9 FSU — travels cross-country to face No. 8 UCLA. Can the magic continue?

🕙 9 PM ET — Texas vs. Arizona State | ESPN2
Texas (45-10) chases its 8th consecutive Super Regional. Arizona State makes their first SR appearance since 2022.

Six games. Every single one of them matters. Set your reminders now. 📲

[Which game are you most excited for? Drop it below!] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Friday is the biggest day in college softball — six Super Regional openers in one day. Wells vs. Miss State at 1 PM. Florida vs. Texas Tech at 11 AM. Alabama vs. LSU at 7 PM. UCF, Arkansas, Texas all in action. Full schedule above. 🥎 All times ET.

stories.title: Eight Spots, Eight Shots — Bold WCWS Picks Before First Pitch (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
8 series. 8 spots to Devon Park OKC.

Favorites: Tennessee, Nebraska, Oklahoma, Florida, Alabama, Texas

Upset alerts:
⚡ Duke over Arkansas (Deifel 0-for-5 in SRs)
⚡ UCF keeps rolling on the road

Who are you picking? 🥎

facebook.long_form:
🏆 LET'S SETTLE THIS — WCWS PICKS BEFORE FIRST PITCH 🏆

Super Regionals are underway. Two games tonight, six tomorrow. By Sunday night, eight programs will be packing for Oklahoma City and eight will be going home.

Before the chaos, here are our picks:

✅ Tennessee over Georgia
Pickens at home, unprecedented 4-peat hosting. Tennessee wins in two.

✅ Nebraska over Oklahoma State
Lincoln is erupting. Frahm carries the Cornhuskers to their first WCWS since 2013.

✅ Oklahoma over Mississippi State
Wells breaks the record. OU wins the series. Devon Park awaits.

✅ Florida over Texas Tech
Home-field advantage is too massive. Florida wins in Gainesville.

✅ Alabama over LSU
Moten + Briski vs. LSU's hot bats. Alabama's pitching depth wins out.

✅ Texas over Arizona State
Seven consecutive Super Regionals for a reason. Texas advances to OKC.

⚡ UPSET PICK: Duke over Arkansas
Arkansas is 0-for-5 in Super Regionals. Duke is dangerous and has nothing to lose. Fayetteville gets tense.

⚡ UPSET PICK: UCF advances
After beating FSU, UCF has proven they belong. The road doesn't scare them.

WCWS Devon Park OKC — May 28. Eight spots. Eight shots.

[Who are YOU picking for each series?] 👇

---
facebook.image_caption: Eight Super Regional series, eight spots to Devon Park OKC. Two games tonight, six tomorrow. Our picks: Tennessee, Nebraska, Oklahoma, Florida, Alabama, Texas advance — with Duke and UCF as upset picks. Who do you have? 🥎
